I am committed to adhering to all residence rules and regulations and to contributing positively to the residence community if my application is successful.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the councillor about a broken streetlight
paragraphs.text: I write to you in your capacity as Ward Councillor for Section 5 to bring to your attention the dangerous situation created by the non-functioning streetlight at the corner of Block 6 and Ntshingane Road, which has been out of service for the past seven weeks. The absence of light at this intersection, which is used by students walking to the nearby secondary school in the early mornings and late afternoons, creates a serious safety concern as the area becomes completely dark after sunset. Two incidents have already been reported to us by community members in which individuals were approached by unknown persons at this dark corner in the evening hours. We understand that the responsibility for streetlight maintenance rests with the Gaborone City Council's infrastructure department, and we request that you, as our elected representative, use your office to escalate this matter and ensure that the repair is completed as a priority. We further request that the council inspect the other streetlights in our section and identify any others that are not functioning, as a systematic approach to lighting maintenance would prevent these situations from arising repeatedly. Regular public lighting is not a luxury but a basic element of community safety, and we trust that you will treat this request with the urgency it deserves. We look forward to hearing from you within one week of receiving this letter.

paragraphs.topic: Formal proposal for a youth skills training programme
paragraphs.text: I write to propose to the Department of Youth, Sport and Culture a structured six-month skills training programme for unemployed young people in the Kanye area, to be hosted at the Kanye Community Centre. Youth unemployment in the South-East District has been identified as one of the most pressing social challenges in the region, with young people between the ages of 18 and 30 making up the largest proportion of the economically inactive population. The proposed programme would train fifty participants per cycle in three practical areas: basic welding and metal fabrication, bricklaying and plastering, and basic electrical installation, which are skills in high demand in the local construction sector. Each course would run for eight weeks, conducted by qualified instructors from the Kanye Brigade Development Trust, and participants would receive a certificate endorsed by the Botswana Training Authority upon completion. The programme would also include a two-week block of entrepreneurship training to equip graduates with the knowledge to start their own businesses rather than only seeking employment. Total funding required for the first cycle is P185,000.00, covering instructor fees, materials, protective equipment, and catering. I am available to present the full proposal and supporting budget at a meeting at the department's convenience, and I can provide letters of support from the Kanye Sub-District Council and two local construction firms that have committed to prioritising programme graduates in their hiring.

paragraphs.topic: Letter appealing a failed exam result
paragraphs.text: I write to formally appeal the examination results awarded to me in the November sitting of the Botswana General Certificate of Secondary Education, specifically the grade of U (Ungraded) that was recorded for my English Language paper. I achieved passing marks in all seven other subjects I sat and I have consistently performed at a pass level in English throughout my secondary schooling, as evidenced by the school-based assessment records that I request the Botswana Examinations Council to review alongside my answer script. I have strong reasons to believe that either a marking error or an administrative misidentification may have occurred, as the result is inconsistent with my performance throughout the year and with the feedback I received from my teacher after the examination. I request that the BEC conduct a formal remarking of my English Language script and that I be notified of the outcome within thirty days of this letter being received. I understand that a remarking fee may apply and I am prepared to pay the prescribed amount upon receipt of instruction from your office. The impact of this ungraded result on my ability to apply for further education and employment is significant, and I ask that the matter be treated with urgency and fairness. I attach copies of my full result slip, my school assessment records for English, and my national identity card for verification purposes.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the Ministry of Health requesting a mobile clinic
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Mabeleapodi Residents Association to formally request the Ministry of Health and Wellness to extend its mobile clinic services to our settlement, which is currently not served by any regular primary healthcare facility. The nearest clinic is located in Mahalapye, approximately fourteen kilometres away on a gravel road that is frequently impassable during the rainy season, making healthcare access extremely difficult especially for pregnant mothers, young children, and elderly residents. The absence of local primary healthcare means that many residents, particularly those without private transport, only seek medical attention when their condition has become serious, which increases the cost of treatment and the risk of poor health outcomes. We are not requesting a permanent clinic building at this stage, as we understand the capital investment this would require, but we are asking for a monthly visit by the mobile health team that already serves other settlements in the district. This would allow residents to receive basic consultations, chronic medication renewals, antenatal care, and childhood immunisations without having to travel to town. We have identified an available space at the community hall that can serve as a temporary clinic room during these visits, and the Residents Association will take responsibility for preparing and cleaning the space before each scheduled visit. We trust that the Ministry will find this a reasonable and practical request that is consistent with its mandate to extend quality healthcare to all citizens regardless of where they live.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to DWNP about wildlife damage to crops
paragraphs.text: I write to the Department of Wildlife and National Parks to formally report recurring damage to our farm at Mathathane caused by elephant incursions and to request urgent intervention in line with the government's human-wildlife conflict management policy. Over the past three planting seasons, a herd of approximately fifteen elephants has repeatedly broken through our field fence on the eastern boundary and destroyed large portions of our sorghum and maize crops before we have been able to harvest. The damage suffered in the most recent season alone is estimated at P12,400.00 based on the value of the destroyed crop, and this represents a significant portion of our household income for the year. We have reported these incidents to the DWNP field officer at Tuli block on three previous occasions and while patrol teams have been sent, the elephants have returned within days of each visit. We respectfully request that the Department assess the feasibility of installing a chilli-treated wire fence or a beehive fence along our eastern boundary, which are non-lethal deterrents that have been shown to be effective in comparable situations in neighbouring countries. We further request that our farm be registered in the government's crop damage compensation scheme and that a DWNP officer be assigned to conduct a formal damage assessment at the earliest possible date. We are committed to coexisting with wildlife and to reporting all incidents through the proper channels, but we ask for meaningful support to protect the livelihood of our family.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a community requesting a police post
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Shashe River Farms Community Trust to request the establishment of a police post in our area to address the significant increase in stock theft and household burglaries that has occurred over the past eighteen months. Our farming community, which consists of 67 registered farming units, is located approximately 45 kilometres from the nearest police station in Bobonong, and the response time to reported incidents has on average exceeded four hours, which is sufficient time for perpetrators to move stolen livestock across the communal area boundary and into neighbouring territories where recovery becomes very difficult. In the past year alone, 43 cattle, 87 goats, and 12 donkeys have been reported stolen from farms in our area, representing losses of approximately P380,000.00 collectively. We understand that the establishment of a permanent police post requires budgetary approval and staffing decisions above the district level, and we therefore write to you as the highest accessible authority with a request to advocate on our behalf to the Commissioner of Police. In the interim, we request that the current station increase the frequency of patrol vehicles in our area from the current once-weekly to at least three times per week. The community is also prepared to form an accredited neighbourhood watch structure that would work in cooperation with the police if the necessary training and registration support can be provided.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting a sponsorship for a sports team
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Shakawe United Football Club to formally request financial sponsorship from your organisation in support of our participation in the Ngamiland Regional Football League for the coming season. Our club was founded in 2018 and has since developed from a small community team into a registered competitive outfit with 24 active players, the majority of whom are young men between the ages of 16 and 24 who would otherwise have few structured recreational opportunities in this remote part of the Okavango sub-district. Football provides these young men with discipline, teamwork, fitness, and a reason to remain engaged with their community rather than turning to harmful activities. The sponsorship we are requesting amounts to P14,500.00, which covers registration fees, transport to away matches, team jerseys, and basic equipment including boots for our least financially able players. In return for your sponsorship, we offer prominent display of your company's name and logo on our team jerseys and on our club's banner at every match, as well as acknowledgement in any social media communications and event programmes associated with the team. We are a registered club with the Botswana Football Association and can provide audited financial records upon request. We look forward to discussing this proposal with you further and hope that your organisation will see the community value in supporting young talent in one of Botswana's most remote regions.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to a landlord about uninhabitable rental conditions
paragraphs.text: I write to formally notify you of conditions at my rented property, Plot 1147 Unit 3, Phase 4, Tlokweng, that have made the dwelling unsafe and uninhabitable, and to request urgent remedial action in accordance with the terms of our lease agreement. The specific problems I am reporting include a collapsed ceiling in the main bedroom following the October rains, a non-functioning geyser that has left the household without hot water for eleven weeks, and a severe cockroach infestation that has persisted despite two separate pest control treatments I arranged at my own expense. I raised these concerns verbally with your property agent in September and again in October, but no repairs have been initiated and the written request I sent by hand-delivered letter on the 14th of October has received no response. I am a responsible tenant who has paid my rent in full and on time for the duration of the twenty-two months I have lived in this property, and I believe I am entitled to a dwelling that meets basic habitability standards. I therefore request that all three repairs be completed within fourteen days of your receipt of this letter. If no action is taken within that period, I reserve the right to seek guidance from the Botswana Housing Corporation and the Department of Consumer Affairs regarding my options as a tenant under current Botswana rental law.

paragraphs.topic: Business letter requesting partnership with a supplier
paragraphs.text: I write to propose a formal supply partnership between my business, Basadi Fresh Produce, and your organisation, Mmopane Agricultural Cooperative, which I believe would be mutually beneficial and would strengthen both of our market positions in the Greater Gaborone supply chain. Basadi Fresh Produce is an established vegetable delivery business serving 85 household subscribers and four small restaurants in Broadhurst and Bontleng, and our current challenge is the inconsistency of our fresh produce supply, which results in occasional gaps in our delivery schedule that erode customer confidence. Your cooperative is well positioned to address this challenge as you have demonstrated consistent production of tomatoes, spinach, onion, and butternut throughout both the rainy and dry seasons. Under the proposed partnership, Basadi Fresh Produce would commit to purchasing a minimum of P8,000.00 worth of produce from your cooperative per month, collected every Monday and Thursday morning at a time agreed by both parties. In return, we request a 10% trade discount on the standard market price in recognition of the volume and reliability of our purchasing commitment. We would also propose a simple written agreement that outlines quality standards for the produce supplied, the process for handling rejected stock, and the payment terms, which we propose as seven days from date of delivery. I am available to meet with your cooperative's committee at any time in the next two weeks to discuss this proposal and to negotiate the terms in more detail.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the council about an unlicensed business causing a nuisance
paragraphs.text: I write to the Francistown City Council to formally report and request action against an unlicensed and unregistered motor vehicle repair business that has been operating from residential Plot 4459, Somerset East, for approximately eight months. The operation, which appears to involve spray-painting and panel beating as well as mechanical repairs, generates excessive noise from power tools, hammers, and electric grinders from as early as six o'clock in the morning on weekdays and as early as seven o'clock on Saturdays, in clear violation of the Noise Nuisance Act provisions for residential areas. The spray-painting activity releases fumes that enter the neighbouring properties, including ours, and we are concerned about the health implications for children and elderly residents in the surrounding homes. Furthermore, oil and chemical waste appears to be washed into the storm drain at the corner of the property, which is a direct violation of the environmental by-laws applicable to this area. We request that the council's compliance officers visit the property, confirm the nature of the business operations being conducted there, and take the appropriate regulatory action including the issuing of a stop notice if the business is found to be operating without a licence. We further request that if a licence is subsequently applied for by this operator, the council take into account the residential nature of the area and the proximity of schools and healthcare facilities before making any decision.

paragraphs.topic: Letter appealing for community hall access for a youth group
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Mabutsane Youth Development Group to request permission to use the community hall at Mabutsane every Saturday from two in the afternoon to five in the afternoon for the purpose of conducting weekly youth development activities including debate practice, literacy sessions, and personal finance education. Our group has twenty-three registered members between the ages of 14 and 22, all of whom reside within the village, and we have been operating informally under a nearby tree since our founding in 2022 without access to a permanent or consistent venue. The absence of a proper meeting space has significantly limited our ability to conduct structured activities, invite guest speakers from government departments and businesses, and retain the attention and commitment of our members. We are aware that the community hall is managed by the Village Development Committee and we submit this request to you as chairperson of that committee. We commit to leaving the hall in the same condition in which we find it after every session, to maintaining a register of attendance, and to sharing a quarterly report of our activities with the VDC. We also propose that two members of our group serve on a liaison committee with the VDC to ensure that our use of the facility complements rather than disrupts any other activities scheduled in the hall. We believe that investing in the development of young people is one of the most important responsibilities of any community, and we hope that the VDC will agree.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting a salary advance from an employer
paragraphs.text: I write to formally request a salary advance of P3,500.00 from my employer, BotswanaCraft Marketing Company, against my salary for the month of December, due to an urgent financial emergency that has arisen in my household. My mother was involved in a road traffic accident on the 3rd of November and has been hospitalised at Nyangabgwe Referral Hospital for the past two weeks with injuries that required surgical intervention. The medical costs that are not covered by her medical aid, including certain procedures and accommodation-related expenses, have created an immediate financial need that I cannot meet from my current salary balance. I have been a committed and reliable employee of this company for six years and I am confident that my employment record will support the good faith required for this request to be approved. I am prepared to repay the advance through a deduction of P700.00 per month over five months beginning with my January salary, and I attach a signed consent form for these deductions should the advance be approved. I understand that the granting of salary advances is at the discretion of the Human Resources Department and I respect whatever decision is reached, but I appeal to your consideration of my circumstances and my length of service in making that decision. Please do not hesitate to contact me directly if any additional information or documentation is required.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the BEC requesting re-marking of a paper
paragraphs.text: I write to formally request the remarking of my Mathematics paper from the 2025 BGCSE examination, reference number 2025-MAT-GM-4419, in which I received a grade of D, which I believe does not accurately reflect my preparation and performance in that subject. Throughout the year, my continuous assessment marks in Mathematics averaged at a B+ level and I completed all prescribed coursework to a standard that my teacher has confirmed in writing. I left the examination hall in November feeling confident that I had answered correctly the majority of the questions and I was therefore surprised and distressed when the D grade was published in January. I have carefully checked my calculation and am aware that the difference between a C and a D in Mathematics at BGCSE level will significantly affect my eligibility for the specific tertiary programme I have been accepted to conditionally. I therefore respectfully request a formal remarking of my paper and ask that the original script be reviewed by a senior examiner who has not been involved in the initial marking of my paper. I enclose the prescribed remarking application form, a copy of my full result slip, a letter from my Mathematics teacher, and the remarking fee receipt in the amount of P250.00. I kindly request that your office confirm receipt of this application and provide an estimated timeline for the remarking process.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a farmer requesting drought relief assistance
paragraphs.text: I write to the Kgatleng District Administration to formally apply for drought relief assistance following the complete failure of my sorghum and maize crops at my lands in Artesia for the second consecutive planting season. The cumulative rainfall at this location between November 2024 and March 2025 was estimated at less than 60% of the historical average, and the crops that were planted in November never reached a harvestable stage, withering before the pollination stage was reached. My household, which consists of myself, my wife, and four children between the ages of 3 and 14, is entirely dependent on rain-fed crop production for our staple food supply, as I am not employed in any income-generating activity. The loss of two consecutive harvests has exhausted our grain reserves and I can no longer provide adequate food for my children without external assistance. I am applying specifically for inclusion in the Drought Relief Feeding Programme and for access to the subsidised seed and ploughing assistance that was made available in the 2022 drought season. I have previously been assessed and registered as a subsistence farmer under the 2022 relief programme, and my registration number is KG-44-SUB-2022, which I trust can be verified in your records. I respectfully request that a field officer be dispatched to confirm the extent of the crop failure and to process my registration for the current round of drought relief without further delay.

paragraphs.topic: Formal objection to a development application
paragraphs.text: I write as a property owner at Plot 2218, Phakalane, to formally object to the application by Sunrise Developments (Pty) Ltd to rezone Plot 2222 Phakalane from residential to commercial use, as listed in the public notices published in the Botswana Gazette of the 14th of February 2025. My objection is based on several grounds that I respectfully submit for the consideration of the Land Tribunal in its review of this application. First, the rezoning of a residential plot in a predominantly residential area to commercial use will fundamentally alter the character of the neighbourhood in a manner that is inconsistent with the approved land use plan for this part of Phakalane. Second, the proposed development, which includes a petrol filling station according to the developer's public advertisement, will generate noise and traffic at levels that are incompatible with residential occupation and will depress the value of surrounding properties. Third, the existing road access to Plot 2222 is through a residential street that is not designed to accommodate the volume of commercial traffic a filling station would generate. I request that the Land Tribunal hold a public hearing in terms of the Town and Country Planning Act before making a decision, so that all affected residents have the opportunity to present their objections directly. I am available to provide additional supporting documentation and to attend any hearing that may be scheduled.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to LEA requesting entrepreneurship support
paragraphs.text: I write to the Local Enterprise Authority to request business development support for my small bakery business, Lesedi Baked Goods, which I have operated from my home in Ramotswa for the past two years. The business currently produces and sells vetkoek, scones, bread, and celebration cakes to individual customers and to two local schools that place regular orders for events and fundraisers. The business has grown consistently over the two years of its operation, and I am now at the stage where I need to formalise and scale it in order to meet the growing demand and to secure larger institutional clients such as hospitals, government offices, and supermarkets. However, I lack the formal business skills and the regulatory knowledge required to take the business from this informal home-based stage to a properly registered, licenced, and commercially viable operation. I am requesting mentorship support from an LEA business advisor who can help me develop a formal business plan, navigate the business registration process, meet the food safety certification requirements, and develop a marketing strategy for institutional clients. I am also interested in the possibility of attending any relevant LEA training programmes that are available to food production businesses in the South-East District. I am a dedicated entrepreneur with a clear vision for where I want this business to go and I am committed to making the most of any support that the LEA is able to provide.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting a plot for a community garden
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Metsimotlhabe Women's Development Group to request the temporary allocation of a small piece of council land within the village boundary for the purpose of establishing a community vegetable garden. Our group consists of 18 women, most of whom are the primary caregivers in their households and are seeking a productive use of their time that will supplement the nutritional needs of their families. We have identified a suitable unused piece of land adjacent to the community borehole on the northern edge of the village, which has good sun exposure, flat terrain, and proximity to a water source. The land in question does not appear to have any current or planned use by the council, and we believe that a community garden would represent a far more beneficial use of this space than its current condition as bare unproductive land. We request a temporary land use permit for a period of three years, renewable upon demonstration of the garden's success, which we believe is sufficient time to prove the project's viability and community benefit. Our group will take full responsibility for the fencing, preparation, and maintenance of the site, and any produce surplus to our members' needs will be made available for sale within the community at affordable prices. We are prepared to present our project plan in detail to the Land Board or to any committee that the council designates to assess this request.

paragraphs.topic: Formal letter of complaint about a government service
paragraphs.text: I write to lodge a formal complaint against the conduct of an official at the Gaborone Department of Civil and National Registration office on the 15th of January 2026, regarding my application for a replacement national identity document. I had attended the office with all the required documents as listed on the departmental website, including my birth certificate, passport-size photographs, and a police affidavit confirming the loss of my previous identity card. Despite presenting all the required documentation, I was informed by an officer named Onkutlwe Sethibe that I was required to provide an additional document that is not listed anywhere in the official guidance, namely a sworn statement from a village headman, which would require me to travel to my home village at considerable expense and inconvenience. When I asked to see the written policy requiring this document, the officer declined to provide it and instead became dismissive and unhelpful in a manner that I found disrespectful and inconsistent with the standards of conduct expected of a public servant. I am requesting a formal investigation into this matter and a written clarification from the Director of Civil and National Registration on whether the document requested by the officer is in fact required by current policy. I am also requesting an apology for the manner in which I was treated. I will be attending the office again on the 5th of February with the same documentation and I ask that a supervisor be present to assist me.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting an internship opportunity
paragraphs.text: I write to formally apply for an internship opportunity within your organisation's Finance and Accounting Department for the period of July to December 2026, as part of the practical training component of my Diploma in Accounting at Botswana Accountancy College. I am currently in my second and final year of study with a cumulative grade point average of 75%, and my programme requires the completion of six months of structured workplace experience in order to fulfil the requirements for graduation. The specific competencies I am seeking to develop during the internship include accounts payable and receivable processing, bank reconciliation, payroll administration, and the preparation of basic financial statements, all of which are skills that your organisation's finance team is in an excellent position to mentor. I am a hardworking, punctual, and enthusiastic learner who takes supervision and feedback seriously and I am genuinely excited about the prospect of learning in a real professional environment after two years of classroom-based study. I attach my most recent academic transcript, a recommendation letter from my programme coordinator, and a copy of my national identity card for your records. I am available for an interview at any time and at any location that is convenient for your Human Resources Department. I would be grateful if you could confirm receipt of this application and advise me of the timeline for your decision.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the council requesting a bus shelter
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of commuters who regularly use the bus stop on Disaneng Road near Disaneng Secondary School to request the urgent installation of a bus shelter at this location by the Maun Sub-District Council. The current bus stop has no shelter, seating, or any form of protection from the elements, and commuters including schoolchildren, elderly residents, and working adults must stand exposed to the sun, wind, and rain for extended periods while waiting for transport. In the summer months, the waiting period under direct sun creates a genuine heat stress risk, particularly for elderly persons and young children, and during the rainy season the road verge where people stand becomes waterlogged and muddy. The volume of people using this stop is very high, particularly during the morning and afternoon school transport peaks, and a shelter would make a meaningful and immediate difference to the comfort and safety of a large number of residents. We are aware that the council has installed shelters at several other stops in the district and we ask that this location be included in the next batch of shelter installations. We are also prepared to contribute labour through a community work day to prepare the ground and assist with the installation if this would accelerate the process. We attach a photograph of the current conditions at the stop for the council's reference.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a ward to request a community meeting with the council
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the residents of Ledumang Ward 12 to formally request that the Gaborone City Council schedule a community engagement meeting with our ward to discuss a number of concerns that residents have been unable to raise through informal channels. The concerns that residents wish to address include the delayed construction of the promised community sports court, the irregular refuse collection service over the past three months, the safety of the pedestrian path along the main drainage channel, and the process for reporting street light faults. Residents have attempted to reach the ward office on several occasions by telephone and by visiting in person, but have been unable to obtain satisfactory information or commitments from available staff. We believe that a single well-structured engagement meeting, attended by the relevant council departments, would be more effective than the current situation of unresolved queries and growing frustration in the community. We propose that the meeting be held on a Saturday morning to maximise the number of residents who can attend, and we request that representatives from the City Engineer's Department, the Refuse Services Division, and the Electrical Services Section all be present. We are prepared to assist with organising the venue and notifying community members once a date has been confirmed. We look forward to your response and to the opportunity to engage directly with the council on these matters.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to an employer explaining an absence from work
paragraphs.text: I write to formally explain my absence from work from Monday the 9th to Wednesday the 11th of February 2026, which I am aware falls outside the two days of sick leave that can be taken without prior approval under the company's leave policy. On the morning of Sunday the 8th of February, my daughter of four years developed a high fever and difficulty breathing and we took her to the emergency department at Scottish Livingstone Hospital in Molepolole, where she was admitted for observation and treatment of a chest infection. As her primary caregiver and as the only adult present in our household during this period due to my husband being away for work, I was unable to arrange alternative supervision for my daughter and had no choice but to remain at the hospital until she was discharged on the afternoon of Wednesday the 11th. I enclose a copy of the hospital admission and discharge paperwork as evidence of the circumstances. I deeply regret the inconvenience my absence caused to the team and I am prepared to make up for the lost time by working on the coming Saturday if this would be acceptable to management. I have also briefed my colleague Mma Setlhare fully on the status of all pending work and I am pleased to report that no client-facing tasks were missed during my absence. I ask for your understanding given the circumstances and hope that this detailed explanation will be accepted in lieu of the formal leave application that I was unable to submit in advance.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting the extension of a lease for a business premises
paragraphs.text: I write to request the renewal and extension of the lease agreement for my business premises at Shop 4, Riverwalk Arcade, Gaborone, which is due to expire on the 30th of April 2026. My business, Kgosi Tailors, has occupied this space for the past four years under a series of annual lease agreements that have been consistently honoured, and I wish to express my strong desire to continue as a tenant at this location for a further period of three years. The business has developed a loyal customer base in this area and a change of location would cause significant disruption to operations and to client relationships that have taken years to build. I am aware that rental rates in commercial areas have increased in recent years and I am prepared to accept a market-related adjustment to the monthly rental in line with reasonable current rates, and I request that your property management team advise me of the proposed rate for the renewal period so that we can conclude negotiations promptly. I have an excellent record as a tenant: all rental payments have been made on time without exception and I have maintained the premises in good order throughout the duration of the lease. I would welcome the opportunity to meet with your representative to discuss the renewal terms in person at a time convenient to your office, and I look forward to many more years as a valued tenant in this arcade.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the government requesting parental leave policy review
paragraphs.text: I write to the Director of the Department of Labour and Social Security to formally submit a recommendation that the current provisions governing paternity leave for private sector employees in Botswana be reviewed and extended in line with contemporary evidence on the benefits of early father involvement in child development. The current provision allows fathers in the formal employment sector a mere three days of paid paternity leave following the birth of a child, which many working fathers, community organisations, and healthcare professionals have described as insufficient to provide meaningful support to mothers during the critical first days after birth. International research and the practical experience of countries in the SADC region that have extended paternity leave to between five and fourteen days consistently shows that early paternal involvement improves infant health outcomes, reduces maternal depression, and strengthens long-term family stability. I am not suggesting a dramatic or financially burdensome extension, but rather a measured increase to ten working days of paid paternity leave, phased in over two years to allow employers time to adjust their human resources planning. This change would represent a significant step forward for Botswana's families without placing an unreasonable burden on the economy. I would welcome the opportunity to submit a full position paper on this matter to the department and to participate in any stakeholder consultation process that may be convened. I trust that the department recognises the strong alignment between this proposal and Botswana's Vision 2036 goal of building a compassionate and caring society.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting road speed reduction through a school zone
paragraphs.text: I write to the Roads Department of the Ministry of Infrastructure to formally request the installation of speed humps and reduced speed limit signage on the section of Maruapula Road that passes directly in front of Maruapula Primary School between the two school gates. The current speed limit on this road is 60 kilometres per hour and there are no traffic calming measures in place despite the fact that the road is used daily by hundreds of schoolchildren who cross on foot during the morning arrival and afternoon dismissal periods. In the 2024 academic year, two near-miss incidents were reported to the school involving vehicles that failed to slow sufficiently as children were crossing, and parents and teachers have raised this concern with the school management committee on multiple occasions. The installation of two speed humps—one at each entrance to the school zone—together with reduced speed limit signs of 40 kilometres per hour between the humps, would significantly reduce the risk of a serious accident on this road. Many roads in Gaborone with similar school frontages already have such measures in place, and we respectfully request that the Roads Department treat this section with equal urgency. We are prepared to facilitate a site visit by your engineers at any time to assess the road and confirm the appropriate placement of the traffic calming measures. The safety of children crossing the road is a matter of the highest priority and we are confident that the department shares that view.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing a community recycling programme
paragraphs.text: I write to the Environment Division of the Gaborone City Council to propose the establishment of a community recycling collection programme in collaboration with the residents of Phases 3 and 4 of Bontleng, as a practical measure to reduce the volume of solid waste being deposited at the central refuse site and to create economic opportunities for unemployed youth in the area. The proposal involves the placement of clearly labelled collection bins for paper and cardboard, plastics, and glass at four strategic points within the residential area, from which the recyclable materials would be collected on a weekly basis by a small team of trained youth who would sort and transport the materials to a registered recycling facility in return for a payment per kilogram delivered. The Gaborone City Council would contribute the initial bins and the training of the collection team in partnership with a local recycling company that has confirmed its interest in purchasing collected materials. The programme would reduce the strain on the general waste collection service, reduce littering in the residential area as residents develop habitual recycling behaviour, and create between eight and twelve part-time income opportunities for young unemployed residents. A full implementation plan including a cost estimate, a proposed timeline, and a draft partnership agreement with the recycling facility is available on request. We are eager to present this plan to the Environment Division at the earliest possible date and are confident that Bontleng can become a model for community-led recycling in the capital.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a student requesting deferral of studies
paragraphs.text: I write to formally request a deferral of my studies at Botswana University of Agriculture and Natural Resources for a period of one academic year, with the intention of resuming my Bachelor of Science in Agricultural Management programme at the start of the 2027 academic intake. I am currently registered in my first year of study and I was performing satisfactorily at the time of the circumstances that have led to this request. In November 2025, my father, who was the sole breadwinner in our family, passed away suddenly after a short illness and the household has been left without income. As the eldest of four children and the only adult capable of managing the household affairs, I have taken on the responsibility of ensuring that my younger siblings continue their schooling and that the household remains functional during this extremely difficult period. I am deeply committed to completing my degree programme and I intend to use the deferral year to stabilise my family's situation and to secure alternative funding for my studies, including a pending application to the Botswana Scholarships Agency. I request that my academic record and registration be preserved in full during the deferral period and that the university provide written confirmation that I will be readmitted to my current year of study in 2027. I attach a certified copy of my father's death certificate in support of this application.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting a change in school uniform policy
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Parent Teacher Association of Borakalalo Primary School to respectfully request that the school management committee reconsider the current school shoe policy, which requires all learners from Standard 1 to Standard 7 to wear black leather school shoes as part of the official uniform. While we fully support the principle of a uniform dress code as a promoter of equality and school pride, we wish to bring to the attention of the committee the significant financial burden that the requirement to purchase leather school shoes places on families in our community, many of whom are managing on limited household incomes. A pair of regulation black leather school shoes costs between P180.00 and P260.00 at current retail prices, and many families have more than one child enrolled at the school. We respectfully propose that the policy be amended to permit clean black closed-toe shoes of any material as an acceptable alternative to leather, which would reduce the cost for families by as much as 60% without visibly compromising the neatness and uniformity of the school's appearance. We note that several other government primary schools in the district have already adopted this more flexible position and that learner appearance has not been negatively affected. We are available to meet with the school management committee at any time to discuss this proposal and to share the full written survey of parent opinion that informed this request.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing an adult literacy programme
paragraphs.text: I write to the Continuing Education Division of the Ministry of Education and Skills Development to propose the establishment of an adult literacy and numeracy programme in the Bokaa Ward of the Kgatleng District, where a significant proportion of the adult population of 35 years and older has been identified as functionally illiterate based on the most recent community survey conducted by the ward development committee. The inability to read and write limits these adults' capacity to participate fully in economic activities, to assist their children with schoolwork, to manage official correspondence, and to access government services without depending on others to interpret documents for them. The proposed programme would be conducted three evenings per week at the local community hall, using the same materials and approach developed by the Division's accredited literacy curriculum, and would be facilitated by volunteer teachers from the nearby primary school who have confirmed their interest in participating outside of their school hours. We are requesting that the Division provide the course materials, basic stationery for participants, and the assessment instruments required to certify completion of the basic literacy and numeracy levels. The ward development committee will take responsibility for scheduling, attendance registers, venue preparation, and the communication of the programme to eligible community members. We are confident that even a twelve-month pilot programme will produce measurable and lasting improvements in the literacy levels and self-confidence of participants.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to a bank disputing an incorrect charge
paragraphs.text: I write to formally dispute a charge of P345.00 that appears on my Stanbic Bank account statement for the month of January 2026, which was described as a 'service administration fee' but which does not correspond to any fee schedule I was provided with when I opened the account or in any subsequent communication from the bank. My account number is 9100-4478-003 and the disputed charge was applied on the 15th of January 2026. I have reviewed my account terms and conditions carefully and I can find no provision that authorises a fee of this description or this amount under the circumstances of my account type. I am a salary account holder who maintains a monthly deposit above the threshold required for the fee waiver applicable to my account tier, and I therefore request a full explanation in writing of the basis for this charge, including reference to the specific term or policy that authorises it. If the bank is unable to provide a satisfactory explanation, I request that the amount of P345.00 be credited back to my account within seven working days. I also request that no further undocumented charges be applied to my account going forward without prior notification in writing. If this matter is not resolved to my satisfaction, I will refer the dispute to the Non-Bank Financial Institutions Regulatory Authority in accordance with my rights as a consumer under Botswana's financial services legislation.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting relocation of a noisy tavern
paragraphs.text: I write to the Broadhurst Sub-District Council to formally request that the liquor trading licence currently held by Mmoloki's Tavern, operating from Plot 7745 Broadhurst, be reviewed and that the establishment be directed to relocate to a location that is not within a residential area, on the grounds of the persistent noise and public nuisance it creates for surrounding households. The tavern currently operates until two o'clock in the morning on weekends, and the noise from music, amplified sound systems, and patrons outside the premises regularly prevents residents within a 200-metre radius from sleeping. Our children, who must attend school the following morning, are particularly affected, as are shift workers and elderly residents in the adjacent households. We have made numerous verbal complaints to the tavern owner, which have been ignored or dismissed, and we have contacted the police twice, but the situation has not improved. We are aware that the holder of a liquor licence is subject to conditions regarding operating hours, noise levels, and the maintenance of public order in the vicinity of the premises, and we respectfully request that the council's licensing committee conduct an inspection and a formal review of compliance with those conditions. If the tavern cannot be relocated, we request that its operating hours be restricted to midnight and that compliance with noise regulations be made a condition of licence renewal. We have collected a signed petition from 63 households in support of this request and enclose it with this letter.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to employer requesting flexible working hours for study
paragraphs.text: I write to formally request approval for a temporary modification of my working hours for the period of February to November 2026, during which I will be completing the final year of my Bachelor of Commerce degree by part-time study at the University of Botswana. I am employed as an Administrative Assistant in the Operations Department and my current contracted hours are Monday to Friday from 7:30 in the morning to 4:30 in the afternoon. My evening lectures at the university are scheduled on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 5:00 to 8:00 in the evening, and I am respectfully requesting permission to adjust my working hours on those two days to start at 7:00 and finish at 3:30, so that I have sufficient time to travel to the university without being late. I wish to assure my employer that this adjustment will not affect the quality or volume of my work output, as I am committed to ensuring that my academic commitments are managed entirely outside of my contracted work time. I will also ensure that any urgent matters that arise on Tuesday and Thursday afternoons are properly delegated or communicated to a colleague before I leave at 3:30. This is a temporary arrangement for one academic year only and I will revert to standard hours upon the completion of my studies. I believe that continuing my education makes me a more capable and valuable employee in the long term, and I hope that the organisation will support this commitment.

paragraphs.topic: Proposal for solar lighting in a village
paragraphs.text: I write to the Kgosi and Village Development Committee of Shadishadi to propose the installation of solar-powered streetlights along the main village road as a means of improving the safety and quality of life for all residents, particularly those who travel by foot in the evening hours. The absence of any artificial lighting on the main road that runs from the bus stop to the school and the clinic means that pedestrians, including schoolchildren attending evening revision sessions and patients travelling to the after-hours clinic, must navigate the road in complete darkness for much of the year. The proposed installation involves twelve solar streetlights placed at 50-metre intervals along the 600-metre section of road, each unit consisting of a solar panel, a battery, an LED lamp, and an automatic dusk-to-dawn sensor that eliminates the need for any electricity connection or ongoing utility cost. A quotation from a registered supplier in Mahalapye places the total cost of supply and installation at P86,400.00, which I propose be funded through an application to the Botswana Energy Regulatory Authority's rural electrification support fund, with the VDC as the applicant. The main ongoing responsibility for the community would be a simple annual cleaning of the solar panels to maintain their efficiency, which can be assigned to a small maintenance committee. I am available to assist the VDC in completing the funding application and I can arrange for the supplier to attend a VDC meeting to demonstrate the technology if this would be helpful.

paragraphs.topic: Letter appealing for clemency in a minor disciplinary matter
paragraphs.text: I write to respectfully appeal the decision of the school disciplinary committee to suspend my son, Gaone Tshwenyego, from school for five school days following an incident that occurred on the 3rd of March 2026, which I believe has been dealt with more severely than the circumstances warrant. I acknowledge without reservation that Gaone's behaviour was wrong and I have spoken to him at length about it and made clear to him that it is unacceptable to engage in any form of verbal conflict with a classmate, regardless of the provocation. However, it is also my understanding that this is the first disciplinary incident involving Gaone in the three years he has attended your school, that he accepted responsibility for his behaviour when questioned, and that no physical altercation or injury to any person occurred during the incident. Given this context, I wish to appeal for the suspension to be reduced to two days and for the remaining three days to be converted to a community service activity within the school, such as assisting the groundskeeper or the school secretary, which would allow Gaone to remain in a structured environment and to make a constructive contribution while still experiencing a meaningful consequence. I believe this approach would serve the disciplinary objectives of the school while minimising the disruption to his preparation for end-of-term examinations. I am available to meet with the principal at any time to discuss this appeal and I appreciate the committee's willingness to consider it.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting environmental impact assessment on a nearby project
paragraphs.text: I write to the Department of Environmental Affairs to formally request that a full Environmental Impact Assessment be conducted before construction is permitted to proceed on the proposed industrial composting facility that Botaung Organic Solutions (Pty) Ltd has applied to develop on land adjacent to the Mmathubudukwane settlement in the Kweneng District. Residents of the settlement, numbering approximately 340 households, are deeply concerned about the potential effects of a large-scale composting operation on the air quality, groundwater, and general living environment of the community, which lies within 800 metres of the proposed facility site. Specifically, residents are concerned about odour emissions, the attraction of flies and pests, the potential contamination of the shallow wells that many households use for livestock water, and the increased heavy vehicle traffic that the facility would generate on the unpaved access road through the settlement. The Botswana Environment Act requires that developments of this scale undergo a formal EIA process and that community consultation be conducted with affected residents before any construction permit is issued, and we respectfully request that these legal requirements be strictly applied in this case. We also request that the Department provide residents with a clear timeline for the assessment process and that a community consultation meeting be held in the settlement so that residents can participate meaningfully. We trust that the department will uphold its mandate to protect both economic development and the rights of communities to a safe and healthy environment.

paragraphs.topic: Letter applying for government housing
paragraphs.text: I write to formally apply for allocation under the Low Income Housing programme administered by the Botswana Housing Corporation in the Lobatse area, and I respectfully submit my application for your consideration. I am a widowed mother of three children, aged 6, 11, and 14, and have lived in rented accommodation in Lobatse for eleven years since the death of my husband in 2015. My employment as a domestic worker provides me with a monthly income of P2,800.00, which after rent, utilities, school fees, and food leaves very little margin for savings or for any unforeseen expense. The house we currently rent is overcrowded, as my three children and I share a single room in a back-yard dwelling, and this situation is particularly challenging for the older children who are at school and need space to study in the evenings. I meet the income criteria for the Low Income Housing programme and I am prepared to commit to the monthly mortgage payment that would be required under the scheme, as the payment would be lower than my current rent and would build equity in a permanent home for my family. I have no existing property in my name and have never previously benefited from any government housing programme. I attach certified copies of my identity document, my children's birth certificates, my most recent pay slips, a letter from my employer confirming my employment, and proof of my current rental arrangement. I hope that the Housing Corporation will see merit in this application and give my family the opportunity to build a stable home of our own.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting community first aid training
paragraphs.text: I write to the St John Ambulance Association of Botswana to request the delivery of a community first aid training course for the residents of Mokolodi Village, as part of our ward health and safety improvement plan. Our community lacks any resident with formal first aid training, and on two occasions in the past twelve months, residents have been in a state of medical distress—one a drowning incident at the nearby dam and one a severe burn—while waiting for emergency services to arrive from the nearest clinic. In both cases, the absence of first aid knowledge among bystanders meant that nothing of value could be done to stabilise the patient during the critical waiting period. We believe that having at least twenty trained first aiders distributed across the ward's 150 households would make a meaningful difference to the outcomes of medical emergencies in our community. We request a two-day community first aid course for 25 participants to be delivered at our community hall, and we are able to provide tables, chairs, and catering for the participants throughout the training period. We understand that St John offers subsidised community training programmes for settlements like ours and we request that our application be considered under such a subsidy. We are prepared to confirm a date that works for both parties and to mobilise the participants within two weeks of receiving your confirmation.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting fencing of a dangerous drainage canal
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the residents of Bontleng Phase 2 to request the urgent fencing of the open drainage canal that runs along the eastern boundary of the residential area between Bontleng Road and Tshwene Street, a section of approximately 280 metres in length. This canal, which carries significant volumes of stormwater during the rainy season, is currently completely unfenced along the entire residential-facing side, and children from the adjacent homes regularly play near its edge, unaware of the danger when the water level rises after heavy rain. In January 2026, a seven-year-old child fell into the canal during flooding and was rescued by a neighbour before suffering serious injury, and we believe this near-tragedy must not be allowed to repeat itself. We have reported this risk to the Gaborone City Council on two occasions since 2024 and have been told that the matter is on a list of works to be scheduled, but no action has been taken and the risk has not diminished. We respectfully request that the council treat this matter as urgent and schedule the installation of a standard chain-link safety fence along the entire unfenced section of the canal before the next rainy season, which begins in approximately six months. If a full fence is not immediately feasible, we request the installation of warning signs and temporary barriers as an interim measure. We are prepared to organise a community work party to assist with clearing the vegetation along the canal side to facilitate installation if this would accelerate the process.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a business requesting a government tender
paragraphs.text: I write to express the formal interest of Tshwara Thipa Construction (Pty) Ltd in the government tender for the construction of five standard classroom blocks at schools in the Kweneng District, as advertised in the Daily News on the 18th of March 2026, reference number DRTS-2026-044. Tshwara Thipa Construction is a 100% citizen-owned registered construction company with seven years of operational experience in the Central and Kweneng Districts, and we have successfully completed fourteen government and commercial construction projects in that period, three of which involved school infrastructure for the Ministry of Education. We hold a valid PPADB registration at Category B level for building works and our BURS tax clearance certificate is current and valid until December 2026. Our company employs 42 permanent staff including qualified artisans in carpentry, electrical installation, plumbing, and masonry, and we have a proven track record of completing projects within the contracted time frame and within budget. We are enclosing the full tender document as requested, including our company profile, audited financial statements for the past three years, a list of completed projects with client references, our PPADB certificate, and the pricing schedule for the five classroom blocks. We are committed to sourcing materials locally and to employing a minimum of 60% of our unskilled labour from the communities surrounding the school sites, in line with the Citizen Economic Empowerment policy. We are confident that our submission will demonstrate the quality and reliability that the Ministry expects from its infrastructure contractors.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting funding for a women's cooperative
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Letlhakeng Women's Sewing and Craft Cooperative to formally apply for a grant from the Women's Economic Empowerment Fund administered by the Ministry of Nationality, Immigration and Gender Affairs, to support the expansion of our production capacity and the formalisation of our business operations. Our cooperative was established in 2021 and currently has 16 members, all of whom are women from the Letlhakeng Village and its surrounding farms. We produce traditional and contemporary fabric products including school uniforms, traditional dress, aprons, oven gloves, and customised fabric bags, and we supply these products to schools, local shops, and individual customers in the district. The grant we are requesting totals P55,000.00 and would be used for three specific purposes: the purchase of four additional industrial sewing machines to increase our production capacity by approximately 60%, the cost of formal business registration including a bank account and a trading licence, and a two-day marketing and pricing skills workshop delivered by a business advisor. With this investment, we project that the cooperative will increase its monthly revenue from the current average of P12,000.00 to approximately P30,000.00 within eight months of the improvements being implemented. This increase in revenue would significantly improve the incomes of all 16 members, most of whom are the sole breadwinners in their households. A full project proposal including our current accounts, a member register, and supporting photographs of our work are enclosed with this application.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the Minister requesting review of a policy affecting rural students
paragraphs.text: I write with the deepest respect to the Honourable Minister of Education and Skills Development to request a review of the policy governing the allocation of government school transport to students in remote areas, which in its current form is causing severe educational disadvantage to learners in settlements beyond the 15-kilometre radius that qualifies for transport provision. I write as the chair of the Xhumaga Parents Committee, whose children attend Xhumaga Community Junior School in the Boteti Sub-District, a school that falls within the transport eligibility zone, but whose feeder settlements of Kumaga and Khwee lie between 18 and 22 kilometres from the school and are therefore excluded from the transport scheme by a margin that appears to be arbitrary in its application. The children from these settlements, who number 43, must either board with relatives closer to the school at significant family cost and emotional hardship, or walk distances of up to 22 kilometres along unlit and at times flooded roads, which is dangerous and plainly incompatible with a child's ability to learn. We respectfully request that the Ministry review the eligibility criteria for transport provision with specific attention to situations where a settlement falls marginally outside the qualifying radius but where no alternative transport solution exists. We further request that a temporary interim bus stop arrangement be approved for Kumaga and Khwee while the policy review is conducted. The children affected are citizens of Botswana with an equal right to education, and we trust that the Ministry shares our commitment to ensuring that geography does not determine the quality of a child's educational opportunity.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing a neighbourhood watch scheme
paragraphs.text: I write to the Officer Commanding Gaborone Central Police Station to propose the establishment of a formal neighbourhood watch scheme for the residents of Naledi Zones 1 and 2, in partnership with the police station, as a community response to the rising number of residential burglaries and vehicle break-ins in the area over the past eight months. Residents of these zones have experienced a documented 35% increase in property crime compared to the same period in 2024, and there is a strong and organised willingness among homeowners and tenants to take an active role in crime prevention in cooperation with the police. The proposed scheme would involve the voluntary registration of participating households, the designation of block coordinators who would maintain contact with a police liaison officer, the organisation of a WhatsApp communication network for rapid sharing of suspicious activity reports, and a monthly meeting between the police and community representatives to share information and coordinate responses. We are aware that the Botswana Police Service has a formal community policing framework and we are requesting guidance and support from the station in establishing the scheme in accordance with that framework. We are also requesting that one designated officer from the station attend our monthly meetings to ensure continuity of the police-community relationship. The community is ready to invest time and energy in this initiative and we are confident that with police partnership, a well-run neighbourhood watch can produce a meaningful reduction in crime in our area.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting accountability for missing bursary payments
paragraphs.text: I write to the Director of the Botswana Scholarships Agency to formally request an urgent investigation into the non-payment of my student living allowance for the months of October, November, and December 2025, which has placed me in a situation of significant financial hardship during the final months of my studies. I am a sponsored student at Limkokwing University of Creative Technology, studying for a Diploma in Graphic Design, student reference number BSA-2023-GD-5511, and my sponsorship agreement entitles me to a monthly living allowance of P1,650.00 to cover accommodation, transport, and food during my studies. The payments for October, November, and December were not received into my account despite my submitting the required monthly attendance confirmation through the BSA online portal within the prescribed time each month. I contacted the BSA student helpline on three occasions during this period and was told on each occasion that my case was 'being processed', but no payment was ever made. As a result, I fell behind on my room rental and was forced to borrow money from family members to cover my food expenses, which has caused distress to both myself and my family. I request a formal written explanation of why the payments were not made, confirmation of when the outstanding amount of P4,950.00 will be paid into my account, and assurance that measures have been put in place to prevent a recurrence. I also request acknowledgement of this letter within five working days.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting clinic services in a farm school area
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the parents and caregivers of learners at Tshimoyapula Farm School to request that the District Health Management Team include our school community in the annual school health outreach programme, which we have been informed is conducted each year at registered primary schools in the district. Tshimoyapula Farm School currently serves 87 learners from nine surrounding farms, and while the school is registered with the Ministry of Education, it does not receive visits from any health service, meaning that the children's health is not regularly screened and they do not benefit from the vision, hearing, or dental checks that learners at government schools receive annually. Many of the children at our school come from families that do not visit the clinic regularly due to the distance involved, and early health problems such as poor vision, malnutrition, or hearing loss that could easily be addressed with early detection are instead going unidentified until they affect the child's academic performance and wellbeing. We ask that the District Health Management Team include Tshimoyapula Farm School in its school outreach schedule at least once per academic year and provide the standard health screening services to all enrolled learners. We commit to organising parental consent forms in advance, preparing a suitable space within the school for screenings, and ensuring that all learners are present on the agreed visit date. We are confident that the district team will see the value in extending this essential service to children who need it just as much as those in town.

paragraphs.topic: Letter applying for a trade licence to sell traditional medicine
paragraphs.text: I write to formally apply for a trading licence to operate as a traditional medicine vendor from a fixed stall at the Palapye open market, in accordance with the Traditional Medicine registration framework currently administered through the Ministry of Health and Wellness. I am a registered traditional health practitioner, registration number THPB-2019-0441, recognised by the Traditional Health Practitioners Council of Botswana, and I have been practicing and providing traditional medicine consultations and remedies to clients in the Palapye area for six years. My practice currently operates only from private premises without a formal trading licence, and I wish to regularise my operations and make my services more accessible to members of the public by establishing a licensed stall at the market. All products I intend to sell are prepared from plant materials that I collect and process myself in accordance with safe preparation standards, and I do not sell any product that is classified under the controlled substances schedule. I enclose my valid Traditional Health Practitioners registration certificate, my national identity card, photographs of my current preparation space, and a description of the products I intend to stock and sell. I am available to meet with an inspector from the Ministry or the council at any time to discuss the conditions of the licence and to demonstrate my compliance with hygiene and storage requirements. I am committed to operating my business within all applicable regulations and to contributing to the formal economy of Palapye.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing an after-school programme
paragraphs.text: I write to the management committee of Boiteko Primary School to propose the establishment of an after-school study support and enrichment programme for learners in Standards 5, 6, and 7, to be hosted at the school on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday afternoons from half past three to half past five. The academic needs of learners in the upper primary phase, particularly in Mathematics, English, and Science, are not fully addressed within the constraints of the regular school day, and many learners in this group have no access to private tutoring or additional academic support at home. The proposed programme would be facilitated by a team of three qualified volunteer teachers who are prepared to commit to two afternoons per week each, and would focus on structured homework time, targeted revision of challenging concepts, and reading aloud and comprehension practice. The school would be requested only to make two classrooms available on the relevant afternoons and to provide a small amount of chalk and paper; all other resources would be sourced through donations from community members and parents. We anticipate that the programme would be attended by approximately 45 learners in the first term and that demand would grow as results improve and word spreads among parents. The volunteer teachers involved have provided signed letters of commitment which are enclosed with this proposal. We are eager to begin in the first week of the next term and look forward to the school management committee's decision.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to a chief requesting resolution of a boundary dispute
paragraphs.text: I write to you, Kgosi Moroka, with deep respect to request your intervention in resolving a boundary dispute between my family's land and that of our neighbour, Rra Gabaake, which has been the source of growing tension between our households over the past three months. The source of the dispute is the position of the stone boundary markers along the southern boundary of our plot, which my family maintains are in the original positions recorded when the plot was allocated to my late father in 1987, but which Rra Gabaake claims have shifted or were placed incorrectly. As a result of this disagreement, Rra Gabaake has begun constructing a small structure in an area that falls within what we understand to be the boundary of our plot, and we fear that if the situation is not formally resolved, it will escalate beyond what can be managed between neighbours alone. I have spoken to Rra Gabaake directly on two occasions and while the conversation was respectful, we were unable to reach an agreement without an authoritative determination of the correct boundary position. I therefore respectfully request that you convene a kgotla hearing at which both families can present their understanding of the boundary, that you authorise the referral of the matter to the Land Board for an official survey if necessary, and that a decision be made that both families are required to respect. I am confident that with your wisdom and the authority of the kgotla, this matter can be resolved in a way that preserves peace between our families and within the ward.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting establishment of a village health committee
paragraphs.text: I write to the District Health Management Team of the Central District to propose the establishment of a formal Village Health Committee in Lepokole, as a community structure that would strengthen the link between the health facilities in the district and the approximately 280 households in our settlement. At present, Lepokole has no formal community health structure and health information is communicated to residents in an ad hoc manner that leaves large portions of the population without awareness of preventive health measures, scheduled immunisation days, HIV testing campaigns, and other critical health programmes. A Village Health Committee of eight to ten elected community members, trained and supported by the District Health Team, would serve as a reliable bridge between the clinic and the community, conducting basic health promotion activities, assisting in the mobilisation of residents for health days, maintaining a register of vulnerable households with special health needs, and reporting emerging health concerns to the clinic. We understand that the District Health Management Team has successfully established such committees in several other settlements in the district, and we respectfully request that Lepokole be included in the next cycle of committee formation and training. The community has already expressed strong interest in participating through a resolution passed at our most recent kgotla meeting, and we have identified eight individuals who have volunteered to stand for election to the committee. We look forward to working with the district team to establish this structure and to improving health outcomes for our community.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting school feeding programme reinstatement
paragraphs.text: I write to the Director of the School Feeding Programme in the Ministry of Basic Education to request the urgent reinstatement of the hot lunch feeding provision at Makwate Primary School, which was suspended at the start of the current academic year following what we were told was a temporary administrative issue with the supplier contract. The suspension, which has now lasted for eleven weeks, is having a serious and visible effect on the learners at our school, 74% of whom come from households that qualify for the programme on the basis of poverty status. Many of these children arrive at school without having eaten breakfast and, without the school meal, spend the afternoon hours of learning in a state of hunger that directly impairs their ability to concentrate and participate in class activities. Teachers have reported a marked increase in absenteeism and in the number of children falling asleep in the afternoon since the suspension began. We respectfully request that the Ministry treat the reinstatement of the feeding programme at Makwate as an emergency, given the direct harm to vulnerable learners that each day of suspension causes. We also request that the Ministry provide our school principal with a clear timeline for reinstatement and the name of a contact person within the Ministry who can provide regular progress updates. The right of every child to basic nutrition during the school day is one of the most fundamental commitments the government has made to Botswana's children, and we trust that the Ministry will act swiftly to honour it.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing a digital skills programme for youth
paragraphs.text: I write to the Department of Tertiary Education Financing to propose a three-month digital skills training programme for unemployed youth in the Selebi-Phikwe area, to be delivered in partnership with a registered IT training provider and funded through the Economic Diversification Drive initiative. The closure of the Phikwe mine in 2016 left a significant portion of the town's economically active population without employment, and the children of that generation are now young adults who are entering a difficult labour market with limited technical skills relevant to the modern economy.
