The proposed programme would train 60 participants aged 18 to 30 in core digital competencies including computer literacy, internet research, data entry, basic spreadsheet applications, and digital communication, as well as an introduction to online freelancing platforms that can provide income opportunities that are not restricted by the local employment market. Participants who complete the programme would receive a certificate endorsed by the Botswana Training Authority and would be given three months of free access to the computers and internet at the training centre to practise and develop their skills further. The total programme cost of P98,000.00 includes tuition, training materials, a daily transport allowance for participants, and a post-training support coordinator. I am available to present the full proposal and the profile of the proposed training partner to your department at any time and look forward to the possibility of bringing this programme to young people who need it most.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to a hospital requesting an itemised medical bill
paragraphs.text: I write to the Billing Department of Nyangabgwe Referral Hospital to formally request an itemised statement of all charges that have been applied to my account under account number NRH-2025-44987, in relation to my admission and treatment at your facility between the 5th and 19th of November 2025. I have received a total invoice amount of P28,740.00 but the statement does not contain a breakdown of the individual charges that constitute this total, making it impossible for me to verify the accuracy of the billing or to submit the account to my medical aid for reimbursement, as my medical aid provider requires an itemised bill before it will process any claim. I am also concerned that the total appears higher than what I would expect based on the treatment I received, and I wish to review each charge before making payment. I respectfully request that the hospital provide me with a fully itemised bill showing each procedure, consultation, medication, and accommodation charge separately, together with the dates on which each was incurred and the applicable rate. I understand that preparing this document takes time and I am therefore requesting that it be made available to me within ten working days of your receipt of this letter. I am available to collect the document in person from the billing office or to receive it by email at the address included at the top of this letter. I remain committed to settling the account promptly once I have had the opportunity to review and verify the charges.

paragraphs.topic: Letter to the headman about a disputed inheritance
paragraphs.text: I write to you, Rra Molefe, as the recognised headman of our ward, to respectfully request your guidance and intervention in a family dispute concerning the inheritance of the late Rra Tebogo Makwela, my father, who passed away intestate in September 2025 without leaving a written will. The dispute is between myself and my father's brother, Rra Sello Makwela, who claims that under customary law he is entitled to inherit the two plots that were registered in my father's name, arguing that as the younger brother he is the rightful male heir under the family's understanding of traditional succession. I and my siblings, who are my father's three surviving biological children, believe that as the direct descendants of the deceased we have a legitimate claim to our father's property, and that the current legal framework, including the Deceased Estates Act, gives equal standing to children of the deceased regardless of gender. I am not writing to ask the headman to make a final legal ruling, as I understand this matter may ultimately require the attention of the magistrate court, but I respectfully request that a family meeting be convened under the headman's guidance so that all parties can be heard in a structured and respectful setting before the dispute escalates further. I am hopeful that with the wisdom and mediation of the headman, a solution can be found that honours both our father's memory and the rights of all legitimate heirs.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting mentorship for a young professional
paragraphs.text: I write to formally request a mentorship arrangement with your organisation, the Botswana Institute of Accountants, as part of my professional development as a recently qualified accountant entering the workforce for the first time. I completed my ACCA qualification in December 2025 and began employment as a junior accounts officer at a manufacturing company in Gaborone in January 2026, and I am finding that while my technical knowledge is solid, the transition from study to professional practice raises questions about professional ethics, career navigation, business communication, and the development of leadership qualities that are not addressed in a formal qualification curriculum. A mentorship relationship with an experienced BIA member who can share their professional journey and offer guidance tailored to my specific context would be immensely valuable at this stage of my career. I am requesting a commitment of approximately two hours per month over a period of twelve months, either in person or by phone, and I commit to coming to each session with specific questions and topics prepared so that the mentor's time is used purposefully. I am open to any mentor that the BIA recommends based on compatibility with my industry and career goals, and I would be happy to share my CV and a more detailed career development plan if this would assist in identifying the most suitable mentor. I believe strongly in giving back to the profession and I commit to offering mentorship to others when I am in a position to do so.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a community requesting a legal aid clinic
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Molepolole Ward 7 Residents Association to formally request the Legal Aid Department of the Ministry of Justice to establish a monthly legal advice clinic in our ward, to provide free or subsidised legal guidance to residents who cannot afford private legal representation. The need for this service in our community has been identified through a survey of ward residents, which revealed that in the past year alone, more than 40 households have faced legal matters including unfair dismissal from employment, domestic property disputes, debt collection threats, and child maintenance disagreements, and that fewer than 15% of those households were able to access any form of professional legal advice due to the cost of private attorneys. The Legal Aid Department currently operates from the Molepolole town centre, which many residents in the outlying wards are unable to reach easily due to transport costs and working hour constraints. A monthly satellite clinic held in our ward, even if limited to two Saturdays per month for two hours each, would make a transformative difference to residents who currently face legal challenges without any guidance. We request that the department consider deploying one or two paralegals or legal assistants to facilitate the clinic, and we are prepared to provide the community hall at no cost as a venue and to organise interpretation services in Setswana for residents who are not comfortable communicating in English. We trust that the Ministry recognises the principle that access to justice should not depend on one's financial means.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing a school feeding nutrition improvement
paragraphs.text: I write to the principal of Mmopane Primary School and to the School Feeding Programme coordinator to propose a review and improvement of the nutritional content of the meals currently served under the programme, based on the findings of a nutritional assessment conducted by the school health nurse in collaboration with the district dietitian in March 2026. The assessment found that the current menu, while adequate in calorie provision, is consistently low in protein and fresh vegetables, resulting in a diet that does not fully support the cognitive development and physical growth of learners, particularly those for whom the school meal is the main nutritional intake of the day. Specifically, the assessment recommended increasing the frequency of protein sources such as beans, eggs, or small quantities of meat from once per week to at least three times per week, and introducing a portion of locally available fresh vegetables such as morogo, spinach, or butternut into at least four of the five weekly meals. We are aware that the School Feeding Programme operates within a fixed budget per learner per day, and we are not proposing changes that exceed that budget. The dietitian has produced a revised menu plan that achieves the recommended improvements within the existing budget allocation by substituting certain more expensive starchy components with the lower-cost protein and vegetable alternatives. We request a meeting with the principal and the programme coordinator to present this revised menu plan and to discuss how it can be implemented at the start of the next term.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting an extension of time to pay a fine
paragraphs.text: I write to the Magistrate Court of Lobatse to formally request an extension of the payment period for the admission of guilt fine of P1,200.00 imposed on the 22nd of January 2026 in respect of Case number LB-2026-T-0088, which I am currently unable to pay in full by the deadline of the 22nd of February 2026 as ordered. I accept full responsibility for the offence for which the fine was imposed and I am not disputing the amount or the justice of the penalty. However, I am currently employed on a part-time basis at a wage of P1,400.00 per month and I am the sole financial supporter of my mother, who is elderly and dependent on my assistance for her daily expenses and medical costs. After meeting these obligations, I do not have P1,200.00 available in a single month. I respectfully request that the court consider allowing me to pay the fine in three equal instalments of P400.00, with the first payment made on the 22nd of February, the second on the 22nd of March, and the final payment on the 22nd of April 2026. I commit without reservation to making all three payments on the dates specified if the instalment arrangement is approved. I enclose a copy of my most recent pay slip and a letter from my employer confirming my employment status. I ask for the court's compassionate consideration of my circumstances and assure the court of my full intention to comply with whatever arrangement is agreed.

paragraphs.topic: Formal proposal for a tourism cooperative in a remote village
paragraphs.text: I write to the Botswana Tourism Organisation to present a proposal for the development of a community-based tourism cooperative in the Nxai Pan area of the North-West District, to be established and managed by the residents of the Xade resettlement community. The community has a rich cultural heritage including traditional San knowledge of the bush, tracking skills, medicinal plant use, and craft production that is of genuine interest to visitors seeking authentic cultural experiences in the Kalahari ecosystem. However, the community currently has no formal structure through which to benefit from tourism in the area and no recognised means of offering their cultural knowledge to visitors in a way that is safe, dignified, and financially rewarding. The proposed cooperative would offer guided bush walks, cultural storytelling evenings, traditional craft sales, and overnight camping experiences on community land, with all income distributed fairly among participating community members according to a transparent profit-sharing model. The BTO's support is requested in the form of skills development training in hospitality and tourism operations, assistance in registering the cooperative as a legal entity, inclusion of the community in the BTO's approved tourism products catalogue, and introductions to tour operators who programme community tourism products. A feasibility assessment conducted by the Kalahari Conservation Society in 2025 confirmed the viability of this product and the strong interest of tour operators in adding it to their itineraries. We believe this cooperative represents exactly the kind of inclusive and sustainable tourism development that Botswana's national tourism policy envisions.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting a social grant for a disabled person
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of my son, Tumelo Sebone, aged 24, to formally apply for the Ipelegeng Disability Grant administered by the Department of Social Welfare, as Tumelo has been assessed as permanently unable to engage in any form of remunerated employment due to his condition. Tumelo was involved in a road traffic accident in 2021 that resulted in a spinal cord injury leaving him with permanent paraplegia and requiring full-time personal care for all daily activities including washing, dressing, eating, and mobility. A report from the specialist physician at Princess Marina Hospital confirming the nature and permanence of his disability is enclosed with this application. I am a widow with no formal employment and I have been providing Tumelo's care myself since his accident, but the absence of any income for our household has created a situation of severe poverty that I can no longer manage without government support. The grant would assist our family in meeting the costs of Tumelo's specialised nutrition, his monthly medication, and the cost of replacing equipment such as his wheelchair and catheter supplies. I am applying specifically for the Disability Grant at the maximum rate applicable to Tumelo's level of disability, and I request a home visit by a social worker at the earliest possible time to conduct the formal needs assessment. Our address and contact number are included at the top of this letter and we are available to receive a social worker at any time of day that is convenient to the department.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting remediation of a contaminated borehole
paragraphs.text: I write to the Department of Geological Survey and the Water Utilities Corporation to formally report and request urgent action regarding the contamination of the community borehole at Mabolwe settlement, which has been identified as the source of gastrointestinal illness affecting at least 23 households over the past six weeks. Three residents of the settlement, including two children under the age of five, required hospital admission following severe diarrhoeal illness, and laboratory analysis of a water sample taken by the district nurse on the 10th of March 2026 confirmed the presence of E. coli bacteria at levels far exceeding the safe drinking water standard. The community has been instructed not to use the borehole for drinking until it is treated, but has no alternative water supply, meaning that families are currently forced to choose between using the contaminated borehole and having no water at all. We request the immediate dispatch of an emergency water tanker to serve the settlement's approximately 180 households while the borehole is being assessed and treated. We further request that the relevant department conduct a full investigation into the cause of contamination, which we suspect may be related to a broken septic tank located approximately 40 metres up-gradient from the borehole. We also request a written report to the community on the findings of the investigation and the timeline for the restoration of the water supply. The health of our community must be treated as a matter of emergency and we appeal for the fastest possible response to this request.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing a school counselling service
paragraphs.text: I write to the Ministry of Basic Education to propose the allocation of a trained school counsellor to Marobela Community Junior School, a school of 680 learners in the Central District, which currently has no access to any form of professional counselling or mental health support for learners. Over the past two years, teachers at Marobela CJS have reported an increasing number of learners who show signs of emotional distress, including withdrawal, aggression, prolonged sadness, truancy, and declining academic performance, which in many cases are believed to be related to experiences of trauma, domestic instability, and the consequences of poverty. Teachers are not trained to provide therapeutic support and are understandably concerned about the limits of their capacity to address these needs within the constraints of their teaching roles. A trained school counsellor placed at the school for at least three days per week would be able to provide individual and group support sessions, identify learners who need referral to specialist services, support teachers in understanding the behavioural signals of emotional distress, and create a school environment in which learners feel safe to seek help. We understand that the Ministry's counselling allocation budget is limited and we are therefore requesting that Marobela CJS be considered in the next round of school counsellor postings, which we understand is under planning for the 2027 academic year. We can provide school data on attendance, dropout rates, and teacher-reported welfare concerns to support our case if this would be helpful to the Ministry's planning process.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting urgent attention to a dangerous school structure
paragraphs.text: I write with urgency to the Ministry of Basic Education's Infrastructure Division to report a serious structural safety risk at Maunatlala Primary School that requires immediate professional assessment and remediation. The roof of Block D, which houses two classrooms for Standard 4 and Standard 5 learners, has developed visible cracks in the main supporting beam on the northern end and the ceiling boards in both classrooms have begun to bow downward, with debris falling during strong winds. The head teacher reported this matter to the Sub-District Education office on the 5th of February 2026 and was told that an inspection would be arranged within two weeks, but as of today, the 14th of March 2026, no inspector has visited the school and learners are still using the classrooms under this compromised roof. We are requesting that an emergency structural inspection be conducted within five working days of your receipt of this letter, and that if the risk assessment confirms the danger, the Ministry authorise the immediate temporary closure of the two affected classrooms and provide alternative teaching space without delay. We are aware that the Ministry's maintenance budget is under pressure, but we want to be clear that we are not willing to accept an administrative delay in a situation where there is a credible risk of a roof collapse causing injury to children and teachers. We attach photographs of the structural damage as visible from inside the classrooms and from the exterior of the building.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting placement on a government training scheme
paragraphs.text: I write to formally request placement on the Botswana Public Service Commission's graduate development training scheme, which I understand is open to citizens with tertiary qualifications who are seeking structured entry-level experience in government service. I graduated in December 2025 with a Bachelor of Social Work from the University of Botswana with second class upper honours, and I have a strong desire to contribute to the public service, specifically in the areas of community development, social welfare programme delivery, and child protection services. I have been unable to secure permanent employment in the social work field since graduation despite applying for several positions, and I believe that the structured work experience and mentorship offered by the graduate development scheme would significantly strengthen my practical skills and my competitiveness in future recruitment processes. I am a committed, adaptable, and community-oriented graduate who understands the demands and responsibilities of public service work, and I am prepared to be placed in any ministry or district where my skills are most needed, including a rural or remote posting. I attach my academic transcripts, a copy of my university degree certificate, a letter of recommendation from my programme supervisor at UB, and a copy of my national identity card. I am available for an interview at any time and can be reached at the contact details at the top of this letter. I am eager to begin contributing to the people of Botswana through a career in public service.

paragraphs.topic: Formal letter introducing a community organisation to a potential donor
paragraphs.text: I write on behalf of the Gaborone South Youth Empowerment Trust to introduce our organisation to your foundation and to express our interest in exploring a potential funding partnership that could support our work with at-risk youth in the southern peripheral settlements of Gaborone. The Trust was established in 2019 by a group of community members and social workers who were concerned about the high rates of youth unemployment, school dropout, and substance misuse in settlements including Bontleng South, Tsholofelo West, and Old Naledi. Over the past six years, the Trust has reached more than 800 young people through a combination of vocational skills workshops, mentorship programmes, sports and arts activities, and referral support to government services. Our work has produced measurable outcomes including a 68% employment rate among young people who completed our six-month vocational programme and a documented improvement in school retention among participants in our mentorship programme. We are currently operating with a budget of P420,000.00 per year, funded through a combination of government grants and a small number of corporate donors, and we are seeking additional funding to expand our reach to three more settlements in the southern part of the city where unmet need is significant and where we have received direct requests from ward councillors to operate. We would welcome the opportunity to present our full programme report and strategic plan to your foundation at a time of your choosing, and we are confident that our work aligns with your foundation's commitment to inclusive community development.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting revision of transport allowance for teachers in remote areas
paragraphs.text: I write to the Director of Human Resources at the Ministry of Basic Education to respectfully request a review of the transport allowance currently provided to teachers posted in remote area schools in the Ngamiland and Kgalagadi Districts, which has not been revised since 2018 and no longer reflects the actual cost of travel between remote posting locations and the nearest town or service centre. I am posted at Dqae Qare Farm School, located 67 kilometres from Ghanzi on a gravel road that requires a four-wheel-drive vehicle for safe travel during the rainy season. The current transport allowance of P450.00 per month does not cover even half the actual cost of travelling this distance twice per month to access banking services, purchase groceries, attend the district education office, or seek medical care. As a result, many of my colleagues at remote postings are in a situation where their cost of working in a remote area significantly reduces their real take-home income compared to colleagues in town, which discourages qualified and experienced teachers from accepting or remaining in remote postings. I respectfully request that the Ministry commission an independent cost-of-travel assessment for teachers in remote area schools and use the findings to revise the transport allowance to a level that accurately reflects current fuel prices, road conditions, and the actual distances involved. Attracting and retaining skilled teachers in remote schools is essential to ensuring educational quality for Botswana's most geographically disadvantaged learners, and the transport allowance is one of the most direct levers available to address this challenge.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting accountability for a delayed tender award
paragraphs.text: I write to the Permanent Secretary of the Ministry of Infrastructure and Housing to formally request information on the status of Tender MEWT-2025-INF-0034, for the provision of road maintenance services in the North-East District, for which my company, Ditlhare Road Maintenance (Pty) Ltd, submitted a compliant and complete bid on the 30th of August 2025. The tender evaluation period as stated in the tender documents was 90 days from the closing date, meaning that a decision was due to be communicated to bidders by the 28th of November 2025. As of the date of this letter, the 20th of March 2026, a period of 113 days beyond the stated evaluation deadline, my company has received no communication from the PPADB or the Ministry regarding the status of the evaluation. I have contacted the procurement officer listed in the tender documents on three separate occasions by telephone and have not been able to obtain any information beyond an assurance that the matter is still being processed. The extended uncertainty creates significant planning difficulties for my company, which has had to retain specialised equipment and personnel on standby pending a decision that has been unreasonably delayed. I respectfully request a written status update on the evaluation process, an explanation for the delay beyond the stated timeline, and a confirmed date by which the award decision will be communicated to bidders. I also request confirmation that the delay does not indicate that the tender has been cancelled without notification to participating bidders.

paragraphs.topic: Final reflective paragraph on the writing journey
paragraphs.text: The ability to write a clear and purposeful paragraph is not a small skill—it is the foundation of every important document a person will ever produce in their professional and civic life. From the simple narrative paragraph that records a Saturday football match to the formal letter that requests justice for a community without clean water, the principles are the same: begin with a clear point, support it with honest and specific detail, and close with a sentence that completes the thought. A student in Botswana who masters this skill is not only more ready for examinations—they are more ready for citizenship, for employment, for advocacy, and for leadership. Writing is how we make our thinking visible to others, and visible thinking is the beginning of change. Every paragraph in this collection was grounded in the real textures of Botswana life because good writing is always rooted in what is true and near, not in what is distant and abstract. The student who reads these paragraphs and then sits down to write their own has already taken the most important step: they have decided that their voice matters enough to put on paper.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting review of a dismissed insurance claim
paragraphs.text: I write to the Claims Review Department of Botswana Life to formally appeal the rejection of my medical insurance claim, reference number BL-CLM-2025-7734, which was submitted in October 2025 following my hospitalisation at Nyangabgwe Referral Hospital for a surgical procedure. The rejection letter I received stated that the claim was denied on the grounds that the procedure was classified as a 'pre-existing condition' under my policy, however I respectfully dispute this classification on the basis that the condition for which I was treated was first diagnosed in August 2025, which is six months after my policy commenced in February 2025 and therefore cannot accurately be described as pre-existing at the time of policy inception. I attach a copy of the diagnostic report from the specialist physician confirming the date of first diagnosis, alongside my policy schedule showing the policy inception date. The total amount of the rejected claim is P18,450.00, which represents costs that I have had to cover from personal savings and that I am entitled to recover under the terms of my policy if the pre-existing condition classification is found to be incorrectly applied. I request a formal review by a senior claims assessor who has access to both the medical evidence and the specific policy wording, and I request that the outcome of the review be communicated to me in writing within twenty-one days. I am available to provide any additional documentation that the review process requires and I am hopeful that this matter can be resolved without the need for referral to the NBFIRA.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting formalisation of an informal settlement
paragraphs.text: I write to the Director of Physical Planning at the Ministry of Land Management to request the commencement of a formalisation process for the informal settlement of Mabolwe East, which has been occupied by an estimated 210 households for between eight and twenty years without formal land allocation or basic service provision. The residents of Mabolwe East are predominantly low-income families who settled on this land in the absence of affordable formal housing alternatives and who have built modest but permanent structures without any legal standing or protection. The consequences of this situation are severe and include the absence of piped water and sanitation services, the inability of residents to access mortgage finance or government housing improvement programmes, the vulnerability of households to arbitrary eviction, and the impossibility of conducting any formal commercial activity from a property without a recognised address. We are aware that the government's National Settlement Policy provides for the assessment and possible formalisation of long-standing informal settlements where the residents demonstrate genuine need and have made significant investment in their plots, and we respectfully request that Mabolwe East be assessed under this policy. We are prepared to work with the Ministry and the Land Board to provide a full household survey, a community map, and any other information required to support the assessment, and we request a meeting with the responsible officer at your earliest convenience to initiate the process.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting proper signage on a dangerous road curve
paragraphs.text: I write to the Roads Safety Division of the Ministry of Infrastructure and Housing to formally request the installation of reflective warning signage and road markings on the dangerous curve on the A1 highway approximately 12 kilometres south of Mahalapye, known locally as the Mookane curve. This section of road has been the site of four serious road traffic accidents in the past eighteen months, including two fatalities, and is widely recognised by frequent users of the A1 highway as one of the most dangerous points on the route between Gaborone and Francistown. The primary risk factor is the combination of a sharp and poorly visible curve with inadequate warning signage, meaning that drivers travelling at highway speed are not given sufficient advance notice to reduce their speed before entering the curve. We are requesting the installation of advance warning signs at 500 metres and 200 metres before the curve on both approaches, the installation of reflective cat's eye road markings along the curve itself, and the painting of a clear centre line and edge lines that are visible both day and night. We are aware that the Road Safety Council conducts road safety audits of high-accident sections of the national road network and we respectfully request that the Mookane curve be included in the next scheduled audit cycle as a matter of priority. Every accident on this stretch of road is a preventable tragedy and we trust that the Ministry will act before more lives are lost.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a parent questioning examination administration
paragraphs.text: I write to the Registrar of the Botswana Examinations Council to formally question the administration of the Form 5 BGCSE English Language examination held at Tonota Secondary School on the 12th of November 2025, and to request a formal explanation and investigation of the circumstances reported to me by my daughter and corroborated by three other learners from the same school. The learners have reported that the invigilator responsible for their examination hall was absent from the room for a period of approximately forty minutes during the morning examination session, during which time the room was unmonitored and learners were left without supervision in an examination that is required under BEC regulations to be supervised at all times. My concern is not that my daughter behaved inappropriately during the unsupervised period, but that the integrity of the examination may have been compromised in a manner that could affect the validity of results awarded to all candidates in that room, including my daughter. I am requesting a formal investigation into the invigilator's conduct, a written statement from the school principal confirming the supervision arrangements for that session, and assurance from the BEC that the results of learners in that room will not be penalised for circumstances beyond their control. I also request clarification on the BEC's procedure for investigating and documenting examination irregularities of this nature, and the timeline within which such investigations are normally concluded.

paragraphs.topic: Letter applying for heritage funding for a cultural project
paragraphs.text: I write to the National Museum, Monuments and Art Gallery of Botswana to apply for heritage project funding in support of a documentary film project that aims to record and preserve the oral history, traditional knowledge, and craft practices of the elderly San community members of the Khutse area before this irreplaceable knowledge is lost. The project is proposed by the Botswana Heritage Documentation Initiative, a registered non-profit organisation, and would involve a team of three trained documentary filmmakers working in close collaboration with community elders and with interpreters fluent in the relevant San language dialects. Over a period of six months, the project would capture on film the traditional tracking techniques, medicinal plant knowledge, storytelling traditions, tool-making methods, and historical accounts of the community's experiences of the land and its changes over the past century. The resulting documentary materials would be deposited in full with the National Museum archives, made accessible to researchers and educators, and shared with the community through public screenings in the Khutse area. The total project budget of P210,000.00 covers production equipment, transportation, accommodation in the field, interpreter and community liaison costs, and post-production editing and archiving. We are applying for 70% of the budget from the heritage funding programme and are committed to raising the remaining 30% through complementary sources. We are available to present the project in detail and to provide the full project proposal and organisational documents at your request.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting interpretation services at a government office
paragraphs.text: I write to the Director of the Department of Immigration and Citizenship to request that formal Setswana interpretation services be made available at the Gaborone Immigration office during the processing of citizenship and permit applications, as the absence of such services is currently creating a significant barrier for applicants who do not communicate fluently in English. On a recent visit to the office to assist an elderly relative with a citizenship documentation matter, I observed that multiple applicants were struggling to understand the questions being asked of them by counter officers who communicated exclusively in English, and that this language barrier was creating frustration for both applicants and officers and leading to errors in completed forms. Botswana's constitution recognises Setswana as the national language and the government's own service delivery charter commits to making government services accessible to all citizens, regardless of educational background or language proficiency. The provision of a Setswana-speaking officer or trained interpreter at the main service counter during peak hours would significantly reduce the occurrence of form errors, reduce the time each transaction takes, and improve the experience of citizens who have a right to transact with their government in a language they understand. This is not a request for a large budgetary commitment but for a simple operational adjustment that could be implemented within the existing staffing structure through a reassignment of a suitably fluent officer to a customer-facing interpretation role during designated hours.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting community policing resources
paragraphs.text: I write to the Commissioner of Police on behalf of the Tlokweng Community Safety Forum to request the allocation of additional community policing resources to the Tlokweng police zone, where population growth has significantly outpaced the expansion of police capacity over the past decade. The Tlokweng area, which is adjacent to the Gaborone city boundary and serves as a residential catchment for a growing number of families relocating from the city centre, now has an estimated population of 55,000 residents but is served by a police station that was designed and staffed for a much smaller community. The result is visible in the response times to reported incidents, which regularly exceed one hour for matters that do not involve immediate life risk, and in the frequency of crime in residential areas that are effectively not covered by any regular patrol pattern. We are requesting a formal community policing officer, a dedicated patrol vehicle for the northern zone of Tlokweng, and the allocation of a police liaison desk that community members can visit or call without going through the main station queue. We also request the reinstatement of the community policing forum meetings that were conducted quarterly until 2022 and that provided a valuable communication channel between residents and the station management. The safety of Tlokweng residents is a matter of growing urgency and the Community Safety Forum stands ready to work actively with the police to address the challenges facing our community.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting review of a child's special needs support
paragraphs.text: I write to the Special Education Needs Division of the Ministry of Basic Education to request a formal review of the support currently being provided to my son, Omphemetse Makwere, who is enrolled in Standard 3 at Dipudi Primary School and was assessed in 2023 as having dyslexia and mild processing difficulties requiring specialist learning support. The assessment report from the Educational Psychologist recommended that Omphemetse receive at least three sessions per week of specialist reading support from a trained remedial teacher, be allowed additional time on written tasks, and be provided with a reading ruler and coloured overlay materials to assist his visual processing. In the two years since the assessment, Omphemetse has received only irregular support from the school's part-time remedial teacher, who covers four other schools in the same week, and the recommended accommodations have not been consistently implemented during tests and examinations. His progress has been far below what the psychologist projected as achievable with the recommended support and his confidence in his own ability has deteriorated noticeably. I request a meeting with the Special Education Needs Division to review Omphemetse's current support arrangements against the original recommendations, and I ask that a specialist inspector visit Dipudi Primary School to conduct an on-site assessment of how the school is implementing his support plan. Every child in Botswana deserves access to the education that their needs require and I am asking for nothing more than what the Ministry's own assessment already recommended.

paragraphs.topic: Letter proposing a community radio programme on farming
paragraphs.text: I write to the Programme Director of Radio Botswana to propose a weekly radio programme focused on practical farming and smallholder agriculture, to be broadcast in Setswana on Saturday mornings when the majority of rural listeners are at home and available to engage with agricultural content. The need for a dedicated practical farming programme on public radio has been identified through consultations with farming communities in the Central and Kweneng Districts, where many smallholder farmers lack access to up-to-date information on subjects including soil preparation, drought-tolerant crop varieties, livestock disease management, marketing of produce, and access to government agricultural support programmes. The proposed programme, which we are calling 'Temo le Bophelo' (Farming and Life), would run for 45 minutes each Saturday morning and would combine interviews with extension officers, stories from successful smallholder farmers, answers to listener questions submitted by SMS, and periodic expert panels on seasonal agricultural topics. Production costs would be minimal as the format is talk-based rather than music-heavy, and we propose that the Department of Agricultural Research and the Botswana Agricultural Marketing Board co-produce the programme with Radio Botswana as partners with a shared interest in supporting the agricultural sector. A detailed programme format and suggested episode schedule for the first twelve weeks is enclosed with this letter. We are confident that 'Temo le Bophelo' would reach a large and appreciative audience of farmers and rural households and would make a tangible contribution to agricultural knowledge in Botswana's rural communities.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting compensation for damage caused by government vehicle
paragraphs.text: I write to the Government of Botswana Fleet Management Unit to formally claim compensation for damage to my property caused by a government vehicle on the afternoon of the 14th of February 2026 in Gaborone's Bontleng area. A government vehicle bearing registration number B 4477 BW, identified as belonging to the Ministry of Agriculture, reversed into my parked vehicle at the corner of Bontleng Road and Funda Close, causing damage to the front bonnet, bumper, and left headlight assembly of my vehicle, a Toyota Hilux with registration number B 1234 CD. The government driver acknowledged responsibility at the scene, and a witness, Mma Sehularo of Plot 4471 Bontleng, who was present throughout, has provided a signed written statement which I enclose with this letter. I have obtained a repair quotation from a registered panel beater totalling P8,750.00, which I also enclose. I am requesting that the Fleet Management Unit take responsibility for the payment of this repair quotation and that the amount be settled directly with the panel beater within thirty days of this letter, as I cannot afford to pay for the repair and then seek reimbursement given my current financial circumstances. I also request a written incident report number from the fleet management system confirming that the incident has been officially recorded against the relevant vehicle. I am available to provide any additional documentation or to attend any meeting required to process this claim.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting a post-office box allocation
paragraphs.text: I write to the Postmaster of the Palapye Post Office to formally apply for the allocation of a post office box for my business, Kgakololo Consulting, which was registered under that name with CIPA in January 2026. My consulting business provides small business development advisory services to clients in the Central District and it is important for the professional credibility of the business that I have a formal postal address through which I can receive official correspondence, contracts, invoices, and payments. The use of a residential address for business correspondence is not appropriate for the nature of the services I provide, and I understand that post office boxes are available for allocation to individuals and businesses upon application and payment of the annual fee. I am requesting a medium-sized box sufficient to accommodate A4 envelopes and small packages and I am prepared to pay the annual rental fee in full at the time of allocation. I enclose my CIPA business registration certificate, a certified copy of my national identity card, and a passport photograph as required by the application process. Please advise me of any additional documents or fees required to complete my application and I will attend the post office on any day that is convenient for the allocation to be processed. I appreciate your assistance in helping new small businesses access the postal infrastructure they need to operate professionally.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting clarity on a school admissions policy
paragraphs.text: I write to the Headmaster of Gaborone Secondary School to request written clarification on the admissions criteria and process for Form 1 intake for the 2027 academic year, as the information currently available on the school's noticeboard and through the ward office appears to be inconsistent and has created significant confusion among parents in our community. Specifically, I am seeking clarity on three points: first, whether the school uses a catchment area system for admissions and if so, which residential areas fall within the catchment zone for Gaborone Secondary School; second, whether academic performance in Standard 7 is the sole criterion for selection or whether other factors such as special talents, disability, or sibling enrolment are considered; and third, what the deadline is for submitting applications and whether applications can be submitted online or only in person at the school office. My daughter, Onkaetse Sithole, is currently in Standard 7 at a primary school in the Bontleng area and I am in the process of researching secondary school options for her. I want to ensure that I submit a complete and correctly targeted application on her behalf and it is important for me to understand clearly whether she meets the eligibility criteria before doing so. I would be grateful if you could provide the requested information in writing or direct me to a document where it is clearly set out. I am also available to meet with the admissions officer in person at a time that is convenient to the school.

paragraphs.topic: Letter from a village committee requesting disaster preparedness support
paragraphs.text: I write to the Department of Disaster Management Affairs to formally request that the Mopipi Village Development Committee be included in the next round of community-level disaster preparedness training and resource provision, in light of the growing frequency and severity of flooding events affecting our settlement which sits in a low-lying area adjacent to the Mopipi Pan. In January 2025 and again in February 2026, flooding from the pan inundated more than 30 households in the western section of the village, destroying food stocks, furniture, and in two cases structural walls. On both occasions, the community lacked the knowledge, equipment, and coordination structures to respond effectively, and damage and health risks that might have been minimised were instead allowed to worsen because no evacuation plan, no emergency communication chain, and no pre-positioned emergency supplies existed. We are requesting training in community-based disaster risk management for a committee of ten volunteers, the provision of a basic emergency supply kit for the community that includes tarpaulins, a megaphone, and first aid materials, and technical assistance in developing a simple flood evacuation and communication plan for the western zone of the village. We are also requesting that the Department assess whether any flood mitigation infrastructure, such as diversion channels or raised access paths, could practically be installed to reduce the vulnerability of the most exposed households. We are committed to working actively with the Department to build a safer and more resilient community.

paragraphs.topic: Letter requesting agricultural extension visit
paragraphs.text: I write to the Agricultural Officer of the Mahalapye Sub-District to request a farm visit to my smallholder plot at Shoshong to provide technical guidance on the management of a persistent soil erosion problem that is affecting my crop production capacity and reducing the arable area of my plot year by year. I have been farming this plot since 2010 and over the past four seasons I have observed increasing erosion channels forming across the lower portion of the plot following heavy rain, which carry away the topsoil that took years to build up and leave behind hard compacted subsoil that is very difficult to work and very poorly suited to crop production. I would therefore appreciate a visit from an extension officer to assess the condition of the land, advise on practical soil conservation measures such as contour ridges, grass strips, or diversion furrows, and recommend any changes in cultivation practice that could help to prevent further loss of topsoil. I am available on weekdays and can be contacted through the Shoshong agricultural office to arrange a suitable date and time.
